_comment: Append-only performance history. New entries go at the END of the entries array.

entries.notes: CI fully green. 3 smoke tests passing on all 6 matrix combinations. Foundation in place for Stage 1.
Domain layer complete. Project, Sample, Measurement, FileRef, ValidationIssue all frozen dataclasses with cross-reference validation. 83 tests pass in 1.3s, 95% coverage on core/, mypy strict clean.
SQLite + Parquet persistence with repository pattern. Alembic migrations forward + backward verified. UtcDateTime TypeDecorator solves SQLite tz-naivety. 48 new tests, 131 total.

Nine parsers covering all seven Stage 1 techniques, dispatched by confidence-pick (BaseParser.can_parse returns 0..1, threshold 0.5). Every parser ships with a golden-file snapshot test against a real fixture from D:/Materials-Informatics/data_raw/. ArrayStore writes Parquet atomically (.tmp + os.replace) so cancelled ingestions don't poison the parse cache. ParsedData enforces 1-D arrays + same-length-within-a-measurement; 2-D image content deferred to Stage 5. Default registry registers parsers in collision-aware order; the .xlsx pair (UV-DRS vs Thermoelectric) is disambiguated by content sniff and verified by integration test.

Crawler walks folders, hashes files (SHA-256 with mtime/size cache), and dispatches by parser confidence. Orchestrator wires crawler → parsers → mappers → repository → array_store, builds a Project with a sample-grouping heuristic (parent folder name, walking past generic technique labels). Integration test verified end-to-end on real Dhivya dataset: 161 files ingested in 2.6s, 76 parsed correctly, 84 correctly identified as non-data (PDFs, JPEGs, .docx). Re-ingestion cached path takes 0.2s, hitting the Stage 1 done-criterion of <1s reopens. Bug surfaced by real data: find_parser(min_confidence=0.0) was letting zero-confidence parsers through, making the first-registered parser a spurious best-match for every unrelated file; fixed with a positive epsilon. Repository factory now a context manager so SQLite engine disposal is guaranteed (otherwise leaked handles block tmp_path cleanup on Windows). Stage 1 done-criteria met; ready for Stage 1E (PySide6 desktop shell).

metrics.cancellation_mechanism: threading.Event polled from on_progress callback; raises _IngestionCancelledError

entries.notes: Latos is now a real desktop app. `latos-app` launches a FluentWindow with four sidebar pages (Welcome, Open, Overview, Review), one modal IngestionProgressDialog, and two Qt-free services (RecentProjectsService, IngestionWorker). Ingestion runs on a QThread via moveToThread; cancellation uses a threading.Event polled from the orchestrator's on_progress callback (crawl-phase only, by design). RecentProjectsService is pure Python: atomic-write JSON, tolerant load, filter-on-read, MRU semantics, 20-entry cap. UI tests use a stub MagicMock(spec=Orchestrator) so the suite never touches real SQLite/Parquet — wiring is what's verified. Bugs caught and fixed: CardWidget.clicked signature shadowing (renamed to pickRequested), method `list()` shadowing builtins inside class scope (renamed to entries()), QDialog.result() override clash (renamed to ingestion_result()), real ingestion accidentally firing in tests (added orchestrator_factory hook + stub fixture), DeprecationWarning from QMouseEvent ctor (replaced with direct signal emit), and assorted ruff naming-convention noqa annotations for Qt-required mixedCase signals + Qt-required event-handler camelCase names. Stage 1 fully complete: foundation → persistence → parsers → ingestion → desktop shell.
